concepts.title: Magnetic field due to a bar magnet
concepts.description: Magnetic field due to a bar magnet is maximum at the poles and minimum at the center.

concepts.title: Magnetic field lines
concepts.description: Magnetic field lines emerge from the north pole and enter the south pole.

concepts.title: Types of magnetic materials
concepts.description: Magnetic materials can be classified into ferromagnetic, paramagnetic, and diamagnetic materials.

concepts.title: Right-Hand Grip Rule
concepts.description: To determine the direction of the magnetic field using the right-hand grip rule, point your thumb in the direction of the current and your fingers will curl in the direction of the magnetic field.

concepts.title: Magnetic field at the center of a current-carrying loop
concepts.description: Students often forget that the magnetic field at the center of a current-carrying loop is zero, not weak.

concepts.title: Unit of magnetic field
concepts.description: The unit of magnetic field is Tesla (T) or Webers per square meter (Wb/m²).

concepts.title: Earth's magnetic field
concepts.description: The Earth's magnetic field is a result of the movement of molten iron in the Earth's core.

concepts.title: Curie temperature
concepts.description: The Curie temperature is the temperature above which a ferromagnetic material loses its magnetic properties.
